skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
stem: A naturalist asked a random sample of campers how many hours they spent outdoors during their stay. Based on the responses, the estimated mean was 7.2 hours, with an associated margin of error of 0.4 hours. Which of the following is the best conclusion from these data?

answerOptions.content: It is plausible that the mean number of hours spent outdoors for all campers is between 6.8 and 7.6 hours.
It is likely that all campers spent between 6.8 and 7.6 hours outdoors.
It is likely that most campers spent exactly 7.2 hours outdoors.
It is not possible that any camper spent less than 5 hours outdoors.

rationale:
Choice A is correct. The margin of error provides a plausible range for the mean of the population: 7.2 ± 0.4 gives 6.8 to 7.6 hours.

Choice B confuses an interval for the population mean with the range of individual values. Choice C inappropriately claims most campers spent the exact mean. Choice D draws an impossibility claim unsupported by the data.
